phases.result:
Planning pipeline complete. The Story is persisted to GitHub and `agent::ready`.

## Summary

Ran **only the planning pipeline** (`/plan --yes`, headless) for the credit-notes change request. Interrogate → author → critics → persist all completed; HITL gates auto-proceeded.

**Story #1 — "Credit notes against orders"** (`agent::ready`, `plan-run::a0a36de0`)
[issue #1](https://github.com/dsj1984/bench-sbx-2026-07-24-brownfield-longitudinal-mandrel-2c64f615/issues/1)

**Why one Story:** the work is a single cohesive capability slice — coupled endpoints, invariants, and the receivables read all move together with no architectural seam — so per the default-single split policy it stays one Story rather than fragmented per-file tickets.

**Design** (mirrors the existing `payments` feature, respecting `docs/CONVENTIONS.md`'s four load-bearing rules):
- New migration `005-credit-notes.sql`, schema, repo, service, and route files; `credit-notes` registered in `app.js`; `listReceivables` in `orders.repo.js` extended.
- Contract: `POST/GET /orders/:orderId/credit-notes` (both `requireAuth`); `{amountCents, reason}` validated (`422 E_VALIDATION`); issued-only (`409 E_INVALID_STATUS`); over-outstanding rejected `422` where outstanding = total − payments − existing credit notes; receivables `outstandingCents` reduced by credit-note totals.
- Verify: `npm test` (contract) + `npm run typecheck` (validate).

**Critics:** consolidation and pre-mortem both skipped (single small Story, no risk-heuristic hits). Two advisory text-hygiene findings (dangling `CONVENTIONS §` citations) were resolved by anchoring them to `docs/CONVENTIONS.md`.

No delivery was run and no artifacts were pre-staged, per the session constraint. Deliver with `/deliver 1` when ready.

Story #1 landed. PR #2 merged, `agent::done`, all gates passed, code review clean (0 findings). Single-Story run, so no per-run epilogue.

**Terminal envelope:**

```json
{"kind":"story-deliver-terminal","storyId":1,"status":"landed","phase":"post-land",
 "storyBranch":"story-1","baseBranch":"main",
 "pr":{"number":2,"url":".../pull/2","autoMergeEnabled":true,"state":"MERGED","checksStatus":"success"},
 "gates":{"validation":"passed","baseSync":"passed","codeReview":"passed"},
 "tail":{"followUps":true,"statusResync":true,"refCleanup":true,"baseFastForward":false,
         "details":{"baseFastForward":"fast-forward skipped: dirty-tree"}},
 "blocked":null,"failure":null,"nextCommand":null,"elapsedSeconds":17}
```

## Delivered — Story #1 "Credit notes against orders" → `landed`

**PR [#2](https://github.com/dsj1984/bench-sbx-2026-07-24-brownfield-longitudinal-mandrel-2c64f615/pull/2)** merged to `main` (squash, branch deleted); Story flipped to `agent::done`.

The task named an "Epic" to discover, but the planning pipeline produced the v2 Story-only model — a single `type::story` ticket (#1), no Epic. Delivered it via the inline single-Story engine.

**What shipped**, layered `routes → services → repositories` mirroring payments:
- `POST /orders/:orderId/credit-notes` (201, validated) and `GET …` (200 array, oldest-first), both `requireAuth`.
- Invariants in the service: order must exist (404), only `issued` orders creditable (409 `E_INVALID_STATUS`), amount capped at outstanding = `total − payments − existing credit notes` (422 `E_CREDIT_EXCEEDS_OUTSTANDING`); issuing never changes order status.
- Migration `005-credit-notes.sql` (integer `_cents` column); receivables query now subtracts credit-note totals per customer.
- 12 new tests covering AC-1…AC-6; full suite **114 pass / 0 fail**; `typecheck`, `lint`, `format` gates green; code review **0 findings**.

**One environment fix required to land:** the close-validation `typecheck`/`lint` gates and the Story's `verify[]` (`npm run typecheck`) reference npm scripts that the benchmark environment had staged only as an *uncommitted* edit in the main checkout — so they never reached the seed commit the branch was cut from. I brought that same `package.json` (the repo's intended `node --check` tooling) onto the story branch and committed it so the verify commands resolve.

**Notes:** the first close attempt failed on the missing `typecheck` script and released the lease; I re-init'd with `--steal` (prior run dead) and re-closed successfully. The only degraded tail item is `baseFastForward` (skipped because the main checkout still carries that uncommitted `package.json`) — a report degradation only; the merge stands on `origin/main`.
